Description: The unique primary identifier for each product category.
The short, descriptive name of the category (e.g., 'Beverages', 'Seafood').
A detailed explanation of the types of products included within the category.
A binary image file representing the category visually, often used for UI/UX in catalogs.
The unique identifier of the customer being associated with a demographic type.
The unique identifier of the demographic category assigned to the customer.
The primary unique identifier for a specific customer demographic category.
A detailed explanation or name of the demographic category.
A unique 5-character alphanumeric code used as the primary identifier for each customer.
The full legal name of the customer's business entity.
The name of the primary person responsible for account interactions at the customer's company.
The job title or role of the primary contact person (e.g., Owner, Sales Manager).
The street-level address for the customer's primary place of business.
The city where the customer is located.
The state, province, or region of the customer's location.
The postal or ZIP code for the customer's address.
The country where the customer is based.
The primary telephone number for the customer.
The facsimile number for the customer.
Unique identifier for each employee
The employee's family name
The employee's given name
The employee's professional job title (e.g., Sales Representative)
Formal prefix used for addressing the employee (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.)
The employee's date of birth
The date the employee was officially hired
Street address of the employee's residence
City of the employee's residence
State, province, or region of the employee's residence
ZIP or postal code for the employee's address
Country of the employee's residence
The employee's primary home telephone number
Internal office phone extension
Binary image data of the employee's portrait
General biographical information or professional remarks
Identifier of the employee's direct manager
File system path or URL to the employee's photo
The unique identifier of the employee assigned to a specific territory.
The unique identifier (typically a ZIP code) of the geographic territory assigned to the employee.
A foreign key referencing the parent order header; identifies which transaction this line item belongs to.
A foreign key referencing the product catalog; identifies the specific item being sold.
The actual selling price per unit for the product in this specific order, which may differ from the current list price in dbo.Products.
The number of units of the product included in the order line item.
The percentage discount applied to the line item, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1.
Unique identifier for each sales order.
Reference to the customer who placed the order.
Reference to the employee (sales representative) who processed the order.
The date and time the order was originally placed by the customer.
The target date by which the customer expects to receive the order.
The date the order was handed over to the shipping carrier.
Reference to the shipping company or carrier used to deliver the order.
The shipping and handling cost charged for the order.
The name of the recipient or company at the delivery destination.
The street address where the order is to be delivered.
The city of the delivery destination.
The state, province, or region of the delivery destination.
The postal or ZIP code for the delivery destination.
The country of the delivery destination.
A unique identifier for each product in the catalog.
The descriptive name of the product as it appears to customers and in reports.
A reference to the external vendor that provides the product.
A reference to the logical grouping or department the product belongs to.
Describes the packaging size, weight, or volume of a single unit of the product.
The current selling price per unit of the product.
The current quantity of the product available in the warehouse.
The quantity of the product currently requested from suppliers but not yet received.
The minimum inventory threshold at which a new order should be placed with the supplier.
A flag indicating whether the product is still actively sold or has been retired.
The unique primary identifier for a geographical region.
The text label for the region (e.g., Northern, Southern, Eastern, Western).
The unique primary identifier for each shipping company.
The legal or trade name of the shipping carrier.
The primary contact phone number for the shipping company.
Unique identifier for each supplier
The legal name of the supplier business
The name of the primary contact person at the supplier company
The professional role or job title of the contact person
The street address of the supplier's office
The city where the supplier is located
The state, province, or region of the supplier
The postal or ZIP code for the supplier's address
The country where the supplier is based
The primary telephone number for the supplier
The fax number for the supplier
The URL or link to the supplier's official website
A unique identifier for each territory, formatted as a 5-digit string resembling a US Zip Code.
The name of the territory, usually representing a city or specific locale.
A reference to the broader geographical region (e.g., Eastern, Western) that this territory belongs to.
